canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, "General capabilities" is a widely used construct referring to a model's overall performance across a broad range of tasks and domains, as opposed to its performance on a specific or specialized task. The construct is frequently invoked to assess whether an intervention—such as model editing, safety alignment, or fine-tuning—degrades a model's pre-existing or foundational abilities, with some studies noting that such interventions may "compromise the model’s general capabilities" ("ETA: Evaluating Then Aligning Safety of Vision Language Models at Inference Time"). The most prevalent method for operationalizing this construct is through performance on multitask benchmarks; MMLU is the most frequently cited instrument used to measure general capabilities, general ability, and model utility. A smaller body of work also uses open-ended evaluation systems like the LMSYS Chatbot Arena. While the core idea is consistent, the terminology varies, with authors using terms like "model utility," "downstream performance," and "benign utility" to refer to this concept of broad, non-specialized performance. Some research connects this construct to other phenomena, with one study observing that higher general capabilities correlate with stronger performance on potentially harmful tasks, while another links its degradation to catastrophic forgetting. Several definitions also frame it as a latent, unobservable trait inferred from benchmark scores, representing the model's underlying proficiency in areas like reasoning and knowledge application.

definitions.text: A model's overall proficiency and performance across a wide range of tasks, which is shown to correlate with its performance on specific, potentially harmful tasks like private attribute inference.
definitions.paper: Private Attribute Inference from Images with Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The model's broader performance on unrelated tasks or knowledge after editing, reflecting whether edits degrade overall capability.
definitions.paper: Everything is Editable: Extend Knowledge Editing to Unstructured Data in Large Language Models

definitions.text: The model's overall performance on a wide range of standard, non-adversarial tasks, such as question answering and instruction following.
definitions.paper: Robust LLM safeguarding via refusal feature adversarial training

definitions.text: The overall downstream effectiveness of a pre-trained LLM across diverse evaluation tasks rather than a single target domain.
definitions.paper: Combatting Dimensional Collapse in LLM Pre-Training Data via Submodular File Selection

definitions.text: The broad set of capabilities of a large language model, such as reasoning and summarization, that are not directly targeted by a specific model edit but can be negatively affected by it.
definitions.paper: Perturbation-Restrained Sequential Model Editing

definitions.text: The overall capability of a model across a wide range of tasks and domains, as opposed to performance on a specific, narrow task.
definitions.paper: Monet: Mixture of Monosemantic Experts for Transformers

definitions.text: Broad task competence of an LLM across heterogeneous chat and reasoning tasks rather than a single narrow domain.
definitions.paper: Diverse Preference Learning for Capabilities and Alignment

definitions.text: The latent capability of the model to successfully complete tasks, inferred from accuracy or scores on benchmarks.
definitions.paper: Optimized Multi-Token Joint Decoding With Auxiliary Model for LLM Inference

definitions.text: The overall capability of a pre-trained model to perform effectively on a wide range of evaluation tasks it was not explicitly trained on.
definitions.paper: RegMix: Data Mixture as Regression for Language Model Pre-training

definitions.text: The overall performance and general knowledge of a model on tasks and data that are unaffected by the unlearning process.
definitions.paper: LLM Unlearning via Loss Adjustment with Only Forget Data

definitions.text: The general ability of the model to perform desired tasks, such as language modeling or question answering, without degradation.
definitions.paper: Model Editing as a Robust and Denoised variant of DPO: A Case Study on Toxicity

definitions.text: The overall level of a model's general cognitive abilities, such as reasoning and knowledge, which often correlates with its scale and performance on standard benchmarks.
definitions.paper: Endless Jailbreaks with Bijection Learning

definitions.text: A model's broad capability across a wide range of tasks and domains, as opposed to specialized performance on a narrow domain.
definitions.paper: Data Mixing Laws: Optimizing Data Mixtures by Predicting Language Modeling Performance

definitions.text: The latent general capability of a model to perform diverse tasks effectively without degradation from interventions.
definitions.paper: Controlling Language and Diffusion Models by Transporting Activations

definitions.text: The overall effectiveness of a model on a given set of tasks, which is a key determinant for successful ensembling.
definitions.paper: Determine-Then-Ensemble: Necessity of Top-k Union for Large Language Model Ensembling

definitions.text: The overall capability of a model across a wide range of domains and tasks, as opposed to specialized performance in a narrow domain.
definitions.paper: Scaling Long Context Training Data by Long-Distance Referrals

definitions.text: The overall quality of an ensemble's outputs on a downstream benchmark or task, as reflected by how well it solves the target evaluation problems.
definitions.paper: Balancing Act: Diversity and Consistency in Large Language Model Ensembles

definitions.text: The overall capability of the model to perform standard NLP tasks without regression when optimized for specific constraints like long context.
definitions.paper: Scaling Instruction-tuned LLMs to Million-token Contexts via Hierarchical Synthetic Data Generation

definitions.text: The overall quality or performance of the model in achieving its objectives, as measured by accuracy on evaluation benchmarks, distinct from its computational cost.
definitions.paper: TC-MoE: Augmenting Mixture of Experts with Ternary Expert Choice

definitions.text: The overall capability and effectiveness of a language model that results from its pretraining regimen, which is crucially impacted by data mixture proportions.
definitions.paper: Data Mixing Laws: Optimizing Data Mixtures by Predicting Language Modeling Performance

definitions.text: The latent capability of a language model that enables it to score highly on a diverse set of evaluation benchmarks.
definitions.paper: Improving Pretraining Data Using Perplexity Correlations

definitions.text: The overall latent ability level of a language model across evaluation tasks, inferred from how well it performs and is ranked relative to other models.
definitions.paper: PiCO: Peer Review in LLMs based on Consistency Optimization

definitions.text: The general, pre-existing knowledge and skills of a large language model, which are evaluated to ensure they are not degraded by specialized fine-tuning.
definitions.paper: StringLLM: Understanding the String Processing Capability of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A broad capability to handle a variety of tasks and input formats, acquired through training on a diverse multi-task dataset.
definitions.paper: HMoRA: Making LLMs More Effective with Hierarchical Mixture of LoRA Experts

definitions.text: The model's capacity to perform well across a wide and diverse range of tasks and domains without task-specific fine-tuning.
definitions.paper: Self-Play Preference Optimization for Language Model Alignment

definitions.text: The extent to which a safeguarded model retains general-purpose performance on non-target tasks after safety training and tampering.
definitions.paper: Tamper-Resistant Safeguards for Open-Weight LLMs

definitions.text: A general, transferable ability of a model, reflected as a commonality across a dataset, that can be localized to a specific set of neurons and enhances performance on related tasks.
definitions.paper: Capability Localization: Capabilities Can be Localized rather than Individual Knowledge

definitions.text: The model's broad task performance across general-purpose evaluations, used here as an overall indicator of whether interventions preserve usefulness.
definitions.paper: Composable Interventions for Language Models

definitions.text: The overall quality and usefulness of the model's generated output for a given task, considered as a trade-off against safety measures.
definitions.paper: Copyright-Protected Language Generation via Adaptive Model Fusion

definitions.text: A latent underlying competency that a model must apply to solve an evaluation instance, often spanning multiple benchmarks and surfacing only through solution steps.
definitions.paper: Unearthing Skill-level Insights for Understanding Trade-offs of Foundation Models

definitions.text: The extent to which a model's response provides practical value or utility to the user for their specific task.
definitions.paper: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions

definitions.text: The ability to integrate multiple distinct capabilities across different types of expertise to address complex, real-world tasks.
definitions.paper: Law of the Weakest Link: Cross Capabilities of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The extent to which a model avoids highly undesirable outputs on rare or adversarially shifted inputs rather than only performing well on average.
definitions.paper: Estimating the Probabilities of Rare Outputs in Language Models

definitions.text: The latent cognitive capacity for problem-solving, diagnosis, and planning required to determine a course of action for a given skill.
definitions.paper: A Mathematical Framework for AI-Human Integration in Work

definitions.text: The latent capacity for executing a pre-determined solution, such as implementing a fix or carrying out a planned procedure for a given skill.
definitions.paper: A Mathematical Framework for AI-Human Integration in Work

definitions.text: The overall proficiency of a model across a wide range of standard language understanding and generation benchmarks.
definitions.paper: Low-Rank Adapting Models for Sparse Autoencoders

definitions.text: The overall performance and competence of a model across a wide range of standard tasks, distinct from its specialized safety behaviors.
definitions.paper: Just Enough Shifts: Mitigating Over-Refusal in Aligned Language Models with Targeted Representation Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: The underlying ability of an agent to successfully achieve a given goal, often operationalized as the probability of success.
definitions.paper: MAGELLAN: Metacognitive predictions of learning progress guide autotelic LLM agents in large goal spaces

definitions.text: A capability that a model possesses but exhibits with low probability by default, which can be revealed through specific elicitation techniques.
definitions.paper: The Elicitation Game: Evaluating Capability Elicitation Techniques

definitions.text: The underlying, unobservable capabilities of a model, such as problem-solving or linguistic skills, which are estimated using statistical methods rather than being directly measured.
definitions.paper: MERGE$^3$: Efficient Evolutionary Merging on Consumer-grade GPUs

definitions.text: The latent ability of an AI agent to perform tasks associated with machine learning research and development, such as understanding, implementing, and experimentally validating novel concepts from academic papers.
definitions.paper: PaperBench: Evaluating AI’s Ability to Replicate AI Research

definitions.text: The unobservable, unidimensional latent trait of a language model that determines its probability of correctly answering a question, independent of the question's difficulty.
definitions.paper: Reliable and Efficient Amortized Model-based Evaluation

definitions.text: The latent ability of an AI agent to autonomously perform the complex, open-ended tasks involved in advancing artificial intelligence research and development.
definitions.paper: RE-Bench: Evaluating Frontier AI R&D Capabilities of Language Model Agents against Human Experts

definitions.text: The underlying competence to make progress on open-ended machine learning research engineering problems through experimentation, implementation, and compute-efficient problem solving.
definitions.paper: RE-Bench: Evaluating Frontier AI R&D Capabilities of Language Model Agents against Human Experts

definitions.text: The latent trait representing the scope of tasks an LLM can reliably perform, which determines its suitability for specific agent roles within a system.
definitions.paper: Position: Scaling LLM Agents Requires Asymptotic Analysis with LLM Primitives

definitions.text: The extent to which a watermarked or modified benchmark continues to function as a reliable and valid measure of model performance, preserving both absolute scores and relative model rankings.
definitions.paper: STAMP Your Content: Proving Dataset Membership via Watermarked Rephrasings

definitions.text: The usefulness or functional benefit of a creative artifact to the user, assessed based on task-specific criteria such as persuasiveness, clarity, or appropriateness.
definitions.paper: Position: Societal Impacts Research Requires Benchmarks for Creative Composition Tasks

definitions.text: The general ability of a model to perform tasks correctly and generate high-quality, useful, and coherent information.
definitions.paper: A StrongREJECT for Empty Jailbreaks

definitions.text: Broad general task competence across diverse benchmark types, including knowledge and reasoning tasks.
definitions.paper: MemoryFormer : Minimize Transformer Computation by Removing Fully-Connected Layers

definitions.text: The underlying, general-purpose capabilities of a base model, independent of specific fine-tuning or prompting techniques, which influences its performance on downstream tasks.
definitions.paper: CodexGraph: Bridging Large Language Models and Code Repositories via Code Graph Databases

definitions.text: The finite ability of a model, parameterized by the embedding dimension 'd', to store associations without performance degradation from interference.
definitions.paper: Increasing Model Capacity for Free: A Simple Strategy for Parameter Efficient Fine-tuning

definitions.text: A model's capability or quality assessed through direct comparison with other models, rather than against an absolute standard.
definitions.paper: AutoEval Done Right: Using Synthetic Data for Model Evaluation

definitions.text: The model's effectiveness on a specific, targeted set of tasks or data distribution, as opposed to general-purpose performance.
definitions.paper: TAROT: Targeted Data Selection via Optimal Transport

definitions.text: The latent trait reflecting how well a given prompt enables an LLM to produce correct outputs on a downstream task, inferred from validation error patterns across prompts.
definitions.paper: Hyperband-based Bayesian Optimization for Black-box Prompt Selection

definitions.text: The ability of the model to directly solve downstream tasks when used as a policy model rather than as a reward model.
definitions.paper: Free Process Rewards without Process Labels

definitions.text: The model's ability to maintain its performance and helpfulness on legitimate, non-malicious tasks and prompts.
definitions.paper: Fight Back Against Jailbreaking via Prompt Adversarial Tuning

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, "General capabilities" is a widely used construct referring to a model's overall performance across a broad range of tasks and domains, as opposed to its performance on a specific or specialized task. The construct is frequently invoked to assess whether an intervention—such as model editing, safety alignment, or fine-tuning—degrades a model's pre-existing or foundational abilities, with some studies noting that such interventions may "compromise the model’s general capabilities" ("ETA: Evaluating Then Aligning Safety of Vision Language Models at Inference Time"). The most prevalent method for operationalizing this construct is through performance on multitask benchmarks; MMLU is the most frequently cited instrument used to measure general capabilities, general ability, and model utility. A smaller body of work also uses open-ended evaluation systems like the LMSYS Chatbot Arena. While the core idea is consistent, the terminology varies, with authors using terms like "model utility," "downstream performance," and "benign utility" to refer to this concept of broad, non-specialized performance. Some research connects this construct to other phenomena, with one study observing that higher general capabilities correlate with stronger performance on potentially harmful tasks, while another links its degradation to catastrophic forgetting. Several definitions also frame it as a latent, unobservable trait inferred from benchmark scores, representing the model's underlying proficiency in areas like reasoning and knowledge application.
